stem: A survey of $60$ commuters recorded whether they take the bus or the train and whether they live in the city or the suburbs. Of the $60$ commuters, $24$ live in the city and take the bus, $10$ live in the city and take the train, $8$ live in the suburbs and take the bus, and $18$ live in the suburbs and take the train. How many commuters in the survey take the train?
explanation: Train riders are the column total: $10 + 18 = 28$.

stem: A teacher classified $50$ quiz results by whether the student studied (Yes/No) and whether the student passed (Yes/No). The table shows: Studied & Passed $= 28$, Studied & Failed $= 4$, Did Not Study & Passed $= 6$, Did Not Study & Failed $= 12$. How many students in the survey studied?
explanation: Students who studied form a row total: $28 + 4 = 32$.

stem: A pet store surveyed $80$ customers on whether they own a dog and whether they own a cat. The counts were: Dog only $= 30$, Cat only $= 22$, Both $= 12$, Neither $= 16$. What fraction of all customers surveyed own a dog?
explanation: Dog owners: $30 + 12 = 42$. Fraction of all $80$ surveyed: $\frac{42}{80} = \frac{21}{40}$.

stem: At a school fair, $100$ attendees were asked if they bought food and if they bought a raffle ticket. The results: Food & Ticket $= 35$, Food only $= 25$, Ticket only $= 20$, Neither $= 20$. How many attendees bought a raffle ticket?
explanation: Ticket buyers total: $35 + 20 = 55$.

stem: A club tracked $40$ members by grade (freshman or sophomore) and whether they attended the last meeting. Freshmen who attended $= 9$, freshmen who did not attend $= 6$, sophomores who attended $= 16$, sophomores who did not attend $= 9$. How many members are sophomores?
explanation: Sophomores form a row total: $16 + 9 = 25$.

stem: A bookstore tallied $200$ receipts by whether the customer bought a novel (Yes/No) and whether they used a coupon (Yes/No). Novel & coupon $= 40$, novel & no coupon $= 60$, no novel & coupon $= 30$, no novel & no coupon $= 70$. How many customers used a coupon?
explanation: Coupon users form the column total: $40 + 30 = 70$.

stem: A summer camp recorded $120$ campers by session (morning or afternoon) and lunch choice (sandwich or salad). Morning sandwich $= 28$, morning salad $= 22$, afternoon sandwich $= 42$, afternoon salad $= 28$. What fraction of all campers chose a sandwich?
explanation: Sandwich total: $28 + 42 = 70$. Fraction: $\frac{70}{120} = \frac{7}{12}$.

stem: A survey of $180$ high school students classified them by grade level (junior or senior) and whether they have a part-time job. Of the $100$ juniors, $35$ have a job. Of the $80$ seniors, $52$ have a job. What fraction of students who have a job are seniors?
explanation: Total with a job: $35 + 52 = 87$. Of those, seniors: $52$. Conditional probability uses the job-holder column as denominator: $\frac{52}{87}$.

stem: A gym surveyed $250$ members. Of the $140$ members who use the pool, $84$ also attend group classes. Of the $110$ members who do not use the pool, $33$ attend group classes. Given that a randomly selected member attends group classes, what is the probability the member also uses the pool?
explanation: Group-class attendees: $84 + 33 = 117$. Conditional probability given group class: $\frac{84}{117}$.

stem: A movie theater tracked $400$ patrons by ticket type (matinee or evening) and whether they bought popcorn. Matinee patrons: $150$, of whom $90$ bought popcorn. Evening patrons: $250$, of whom $175$ bought popcorn. What fraction of evening patrons did NOT buy popcorn?
explanation: Evening without popcorn: $250 - 175 = 75$. Denominator is evening patrons: $\frac{75}{250} = \frac{3}{10}$.

stem: A survey of $300$ shoppers recorded whether they shopped online (Yes/No) and whether they used a mobile device (Yes/No). Online & mobile $= 96$, online & not mobile $= 64$, not online & mobile $= 48$, not online & not mobile $= 92$. What is the probability that a randomly selected shopper used a mobile device, given that the shopper shopped online? Give your answer as a decimal.
explanation: Online shoppers: $96 + 64 = 160$. Of those, mobile: $96$. Conditional: $\frac{96}{160} = 0.6$.

stem: A high school offers two electives: art and music. Of $240$ students, $90$ take art only, $60$ take music only, $30$ take both, and the rest take neither. What fraction of students who take music also take art?
explanation: Music students: $60 + 30 = 90$. Of those, also art: $30$. So $\frac{30}{90} = \frac{1}{3}$.

stem: A farm inspected $500$ eggs, classifying each by size (large or medium) and quality (grade A or grade B). Large grade A $= 210$, large grade B $= 90$, medium grade A $= 140$, medium grade B $= 60$. What percent of grade A eggs are medium?
explanation: Grade A total: $210 + 140 = 350$. Medium grade A: $140$. Percent: $\frac{140}{350} = 0.40 = 40\%$.

stem: A science fair judged $160$ projects by category (biology or chemistry) and result (finalist or not). Biology finalists $= 18$, biology non-finalists $= 62$, chemistry finalists $= 22$, chemistry non-finalists $= 58$. Given that a project is a finalist, what is the probability it is a chemistry project?
explanation: Finalists total: $18 + 22 = 40$. Chemistry finalists: $22$. Conditional: $\frac{22}{40} = \frac{11}{20}$.

stem: A city surveyed $320$ residents about two ballot measures, A and B. Supported both $= 80$, supported A only $= 72$, supported B only $= 88$, supported neither $= 80$. What fraction of residents who supported measure B also supported measure A?
explanation: Supporters of B: $80 + 88 = 168$. Of those, also A: $80$. So $\frac{80}{168} = \frac{10}{21}$.

stem: A university survey of $450$ students recorded major (STEM or non-STEM) and housing (on-campus or off-campus). STEM on-campus $= 135$, STEM off-campus $= 45$, non-STEM on-campus $= 162$, non-STEM off-campus $= 108$. What fraction of on-campus students are STEM majors?
explanation: On-campus total: $135 + 162 = 297$. STEM on-campus: $135$. So $\frac{135}{297} = \frac{5}{11}$.

stem: A library surveyed $600$ members, classifying each by whether they borrowed a physical book last month and whether they borrowed an ebook last month. $420$ borrowed a physical book, $300$ borrowed an ebook, and $60$ borrowed neither. Given that a randomly selected member borrowed an ebook, what is the probability the member also borrowed a physical book?
explanation: Borrowed at least one: $600 - 60 = 540$. Both: $420 + 300 - 540 = 180$. Among the $300$ ebook borrowers, $180$ also borrowed physical, so $\frac{180}{300} = \frac{3}{5}$.

stem: A company surveyed $480$ employees about two benefits: a retirement plan and health insurance. Every employee has at least one of the two. $348$ have the retirement plan, $396$ have health insurance. Given that an employee has health insurance, what fraction also have the retirement plan?
explanation: Since every employee has at least one, both $= 348 + 396 - 480 = 264$. Among the $396$ with health insurance, $264$ have retirement. $\frac{264}{396} = \frac{2}{3}$.

stem: A school tracked $720$ students by participation in drama (Yes/No) and participation in debate (Yes/No). $180$ students are in drama. Of students not in drama, $\frac{1}{9}$ are in debate. Of students in drama, $\frac{1}{3}$ are also in debate. What fraction of all debate students are also in drama?
explanation: Drama in debate: $\frac{1}{3}(180)=60$. Not-drama: $720-180=540$; of those in debate: $\frac{1}{9}(540)=60$. Total debate: $120$. Drama share of debate: $\frac{60}{120}=\frac{1}{2}$.

stem: A poll of $800$ voters classified each by age group (under $40$ or $40$ and over) and candidate preference (X or Y). $60\%$ of voters are $40$ and over. Among voters under $40$, $45\%$ prefer X. Among voters $40$ and over, $55\%$ prefer X. Given that a randomly selected voter prefers candidate X, what is the probability the voter is $40$ and over?
